# agent-connector

> Write your MCP server + hooks (and commands / skills / subagents) ONCE; install them across every detected AI-agent CLI in its native config dialect тАФ and get default, platform-independent, local-first per-tool token telemetry.

## What it is

Middleware that solves two recurring problems for MCP/agent-tooling developers:
1. Every agent host re-invents MCP registration + lifecycle hooks with incompatible config files, root keys, formats (JSON/JSONC/TOML/YAML/exported TS), transports, scopes, and event names.
2. No host reports per-tool token usage back to an MCP server тАФ so "which of my tools cost the most context?" is otherwise unanswerable.

Declare once with `defineConnector({...})`; the CLI detects installed hosts, renders each native config, installs/syncs/uninstalls, and measures your server's own token footprint locally.

## Two pillars

- **Single-API multi-platform deployment.** One declarative + programmatic config тЖТ adapters render it into each platform's native MCP + hook + content config; one CLI installs/syncs/uninstalls everywhere.
- **Default per-MCP token telemetry.** Platform-independent, local-first, privacy-preserving (aggregate counts, never content). On by default, opt-out granular.

## Surfaces (declare once, deploy everywhere)

- `server` тАФ one transport-polymorphic MCP server (stdio | http | sse | ws).
- `hooks` тАФ normalized lifecycle hooks (SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, тАж).
- `commands` тАФ slash commands (content files).
- `skills` тАФ Agent Skills (SKILL.md folders + resources).
- `subagents` тАФ named subagents (system prompt + tool/model scoping).

## Quick start

```bash
# agent-connector is an SDK you depend on тАФ no global install required
npm install @ken-jo/agent-connector             # inside the package that holds your connector
cd my-mcp-project                    # contains agent-connector.config.mjs
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector detect              # which hosts are installed here?
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector install --dry-run   # preview every change, everywhere
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector install             # deploy across detected hosts
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector doctor              # per-platform health checks
npx @ken-jo/agent-connector telemetry report    # per-tool token footprint
```

Or ship a branded CLI: `createConnectorCli({ name, connector })` from `agent-connector/cli` exposes every subcommand under your own bin, auto-scoped to your connector (`acme-db install` тЙИ `agent-connector install --connector ./agent-connector.config.mjs`; explicit `--connector`/`--connector-id` always overrides).

## Key API: defineConnector fields

- `id` (required, kebab-case), `displayName`, `version`.
- `server?: ServerDef` тАФ `transport`, `command`/`args`/`env`/`cwd` (stdio) or `url`/`headers`/`auth` (remote), `tools`, `timeoutMs`, `enabled`, `wrapForTelemetry`.
- `hooks?: HooksConfig` тАФ per event: `{ matcher?, handler(evt) }`; handler returns `{ decision: allow|deny|modify|context|ask, reason?, updatedInput?, additionalContext?, updatedOutput? }` or void.
- `commands?`, `skills?`, `subagents?` тАФ content-only surfaces.
- `telemetry?: TelemetryConfig` тАФ `enabled` (default true), `modelFamilyHint`, `measureToolDefs`, `hostNativeUsage`, `store`, `calibration`.
- `platforms?` тАФ per-platform overrides / escape hatch (`extra`, disable a surface, force scope).
- `targets?` тАФ `"auto"` (default, all detected) or an explicit `PlatformId[]`.
- `publish?` тАФ distribution metadata for the official MCP artifacts (`package --format mcp-server-json|mcpb`): `registryNamespace` (reverse-DNS namespace YOU own; server.json name = `<namespace>/<id>`), `packageName` (your REAL published package), `registryBaseUrl?`, `author { name, email?, url? }`. Optional; the relevant format raises a clear error only when its required field is missing.

Validation: throws `ConnectorConfigError` on bad id, non-function handler, missing required field, duplicate surface name, skill description >1024 chars, or unsafe `resources` path.

## CLI commands

- `detect` тАФ list installed platforms, scope, capabilities, hook paradigm.
- `install [--scope user|project] [--targets a,b] [--connector path] [--dry-run]`
- `uninstall [--connector-id <id>] [--targets тАж] [--purge] [--dry-run]` тАФ full inverse; `--purge` also removes registered framework state + the home binary when no connectors remain.
- `upgrade [--channel stable|latest]` тАФ ONE "bring everything current" verb (aliases: `sync`, `update`): idempotent re-render (identical entries report skip), heals the stable home pointer, prints managed-update guidance (never silent).
- `doctor [--targets тАж] [--probe] [--json]` тАФ per-platform health checks; `--probe` runs a live MCP handshake (initialize тЖТ ping тЖТ tools/list) against the real stdio server; non-zero exit on FAIL.
- `status [--json]` тАФ light install-state summary (which connectors are present on which hosts); always exits 0.
- `package [--format <fmt>|all] [--out <dir>]` тАФ emit a marketplace/extension bundle (9 host formats: claude-plugin (default) ┬╖ codex-plugin ┬╖ factory-plugin ┬╖ gemini-extension ┬╖ qwen-extension ┬╖ agy-plugin ┬╖ cursor-plugin ┬╖ kimi-plugin ┬╖ npm-plugin) or an official MCP standard artifact by explicit name: `mcp-server-json` (MCP Registry server.json) ┬╖ `mcpb` (MCPB manifest) тАФ both require the connector's `publish` block and are excluded from `all`.
- `telemetry report|export|leaderboard` тАФ per-MCP token telemetry (`leaderboard --by mcp|tool|surface`).
- `usage report|export|leaderboard` тАФ host-native usage parsed read-only from CLI logs.
- `leaderboard` тАФ unified: ЁЯФМ per-MCP ┬╖ ЁЯЦея╕П host/user ┬╖ ЁЯЫ░я╕П live host-native turns (never summed).
- `hook`, `serve`, `usage-event` тАФ internal entrypoints hosts point at.

## Supported platforms by paradigm (29)

- `json-stdio` (full hook dispatch, 16): claude-code, codex, cursor, vscode-copilot, jetbrains-copilot, copilot-cli, gemini-cli, qwen-code, kiro, kimi, crush, goose, hermes, droid, antigravity, antigravity-cli.
- `mcp-only` (MCP registration only, 9): warp, kilo, roo-code, trae, zed, amp, codebuff, mux, pi.
- `ts-plugin` (generated bridge module, 4): opencode, kilo-cli, omp, openclaw.

## Telemetry / leaderboards

- Default tokenizer: `gpt-tokenizer` (pure-JS); `o200k_base` for OpenAI/Codex, documented approximation for Anthropic; falls back to `chars/4` heuristic. Every row tagged `tokenizer-exact | tokenizer-calibrated | tokenizer-approx | heuristic | host-native`.
- Two axes, never summed: the developer/surface axis (ЁЯФМ per-MCP serve-proxy rows тАФ scopes call|tool_defs|hook тАФ plus static command/skill/subagent context footprints; `telemetry leaderboard --by mcp|tool|surface`) vs the user/host axis (ЁЯЦея╕П host CLI-log scans + ЁЯЫ░я╕П live host-native model_turn rows).
- Privacy: aggregate counts only; zero egress by default. Opt out: `AGENT_CONNECTOR_TELEMETRY=0` or `telemetry: { enabled: false }`.
- Env switches: `AGENT_CONNECTOR_DATA_DIR` (relocate data-root) ┬╖ `AGENT_CONNECTOR_TELEMETRY=0` (kill switch) ┬╖ `AGENT_CONNECTOR_HOST_NATIVE=1` (force host-native turn capture) ┬╖ `AGENT_CONNECTOR_CALIBRATE=anthropic` + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (BOTH required to enable the opt-in count_tokens calibration sampler).

## Extensibility

- Adding a platform = one lazily-loaded registry entry (`src/adapters/registry.ts`) + one adapter class; `platforms.<id>.extra` is the per-platform escape hatch (thin universal core, fat per-adapter tail).

## Links

- [README](README.md) тАФ overview + quick start.
- [Architecture](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) тАФ the authoritative design.
- [Full LLM reference](llms-full.txt) тАФ exhaustive API + CLI + platform + telemetry reference.
- [Example connector](examples/acme-db/agent-connector.config.mjs).
- [Skill](skills/agent-connector/SKILL.md) тАФ agent-facing how-to (deployable via the skills surface).
